subject: Re: Warp Out of Memory Error During Full Tomogram Reconstruction
body:
Hi Rose,
Getting an exact match can be a little tricky because the way the origins in 3D are set is a bit different - using tomograms generated by whatever program you will use downstream simplifies a lot of things :-)
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On 15 Nov 2023, at 09:25, RWilfong <rwilfong24@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Hi Alister, 
Thank you for responding and for your help! I appreciate it! I was trying to reconstruct a bin2 tomogram to see if I could get an exact match from Warp when compared to my IMOD reconstruction, but you've cleared it up for me! 
Best, 
Rose 
On Monday, November 13, 2023 at 3:49:52 PM UTC-5 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
Hi Rose,
In the typical warp workflow you would not reconstruct the whole tomogram at bin2. 
You would instead reconstruct a heavily binned tomogram (10-15Apx), pick in this tomogram then reconstruct subtomograms centered on those picks.
The out of memory error is expected when reconstructing a large tomogram, is there a reason you need a bin2 tomogram specifically?
Cheers,
Alister
On Mon, 13 Nov 2023 at 07:40, RWilfong <
rwilf...@gmail.com
> wrote:
Hello, 
I'm currently working with the EMPIAR-10164 5 series tilt-set and I'm at the reconstruct full tomograms stage. I'm trying to export the tomograms at bin2 (pixel size 2.7A), but I keep getting a System.OutOfMemoryException. I attached the error written into the lastcrash.txt as an image in this posting. 
I did some digging around on this forum and couldn't find an answer to my problem (I might have missed it if it has been previously posted) and I saw that in the TeamTomo tutorial, there were issues reconstructing at 1.35A for subtomograms. 
I have also attached the input used in the reconstruct full tomograms parameters and the input parameters for warp (pixel size, binning, dimensions). Let me know if there are any issues with the files! 
The computer I'm running on is a Windows 10 machine wtih a 64 GB ram and has the following processor: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 6136 CPU @ 3.00Ghz 2.99 GHz (2 processors). There is also a NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 GPU in the machine. 
Has anyone ran into this issue before and/or know how to get around it? I greatly appreciate it! 
Thanks! 
Rose
